priorities.title: Weight placement and demi-pointe proprioception
priorities.detail: Toes leave floor on demi-pointe (bilateral, physio-confirmed), heels gripping to maintain turnout anchors weight backward, sitting back in heels even in fifth. Upstream of pirouette axis, winging, ankle instability, and retiré mechanics. Must be reliable on flat and in slow rises before pointe and jumps can return.

priorities.title: Left lateral chain / bilateral ankle stability
priorities.detail: Left lateral chain weakness confirmed at baseline (present on flat, not just under load). Left side carrying 15+ weeks of compensatory load from right ankle recovery. Drives torso collapse, winging, hip hiking, retiré instability, pirouette axis failure. Right ankle plié range still restricted.

priorities.title: Barre technique and retiré mechanics
priorities.detail: Frappés, fondu, grand battement, rond de jambe all showing active corrections across June sessions. Retiré being built foot-up rather than hip-down: glute not activating, knee not lifting, hip rotating forward. Current restrictions are the window to fix this before jumps and pointe return.

themes.name: Weight placement and balance
themes.summary: Posterior weight bias in every context — barre, centre, pointe, allegro. Toes can leave floor on demi-pointe. Root cause identified June 2026: forcing turnout from heels anchors weight backward.

themes.name: Outer hip and ankle stability
themes.summary: Left lateral chain weakness confirmed at baseline (present on flat). Bilateral ankle winging on demi-pointe. Left side overloaded from 15+ weeks compensating for right ankle.

themes.name: Retiré and passé mechanics
themes.summary: Retiré built foot-up rather than hip-down. Glute not activating to hold knee, knee not lifting, right hip rotating forward, working side pulling back, sickling downstream of all of this.

themes.name: Pirouette axis and preparation
themes.summary: Axis collapses before pirouette finishes — closing in 3rd; arm-driven rotation; shoulder wind-up; passé dropping at 1.5 turns. Progress: good double May 2026, solid singles June 2026.

themes.name: Shoulder girdle and épaulement
themes.summary: Traps dominance and shoulder blades riding up suppressing genuine épaulement and upper back twist. Pilates targeting this from May 2026.

themes.name: Arabesque mechanics
themes.summary: Second shoulder hiking; eyeline down; back gripping at end range (hip flexor ceiling, not back strength); deep hip rotators releasing too early in fouetté.

themes.name: Hip hiking in développés and battement
themes.summary: Working hip lifting in développé devant, à la seconde, and grand battement to the side. Connects to pelvic alignment and left lateral chain.

themes.name: Pelvic alignment
themes.summary: Posterior pelvic tilt mistaken for neutral — improving as of May 2026 but proprioceptive sense still unreliable. Mirror not reliable for assessment.

themes.name: Foot intrinsics and arch
themes.summary: Toe gripping and arch collapse under load in rises. Toes leaving floor on demi-pointe. Bilateral winging confirmed by physio June 2026.

themes.name: Thoracic stiffness and shoulder mobility
themes.summary: Thoracic extension restricted — lumbar compensates in arabesques and backbends. Shoulder mobility affecting arms to fifth. Only starting to find back engagement in arms second this year.

themes.name: Musicality and expression
themes.summary: Finishing phrases early at the barre, rushing. DR: projection and looking beyond arms, musicality as the main priority.

exercises.name: Pliés
corrections: Grand plié in first: don't go so low
Shoulders don't come forward in grand plié
Core engaged, don't collapse forward
Weight even in plié in fourth

exercises.name: Battements tendus
corrections: Toes stay completely on the floor when returning to fifth — lifting noted

exercises.name: Battements fondus / ronds de jambe en l'air
corrections: Fondu: thigh leads rather than heel or foot
Coupé low and on top of ankle
Find resistance at the fondu to demi-pointe
Lengthen supporting side especially in second and derrière
Leg directly behind hip in derrière
Sequence: fondu supporting leg + attitude before demi-pointe
Right foot not fully up on demi-pointe — push up and over the toes
Rond de jambe jeté: aligned in front and behind; timing too quick
Rond en l'air: accent and resistance more emphasised; sharper coming in

exercises.name: Battements frappés / petits battements
corrections: Losing accent and dynamic on the en dedans
Accent and hold not sustained en croix
Memorise the pattern without getting distracted
Coupé under and over: both feet must point fully

exercises.name: Grands battements
corrections: Weight sitting back in devant
Don't curl toes
Don't hike working hip to the side
Don't twist shoulders — square with hips
Passé sickled — glute not activating, knee not lifting
Toes scoop and articulate before passé
Think knee lifting not foot leading

exercises.name: Port de bras
corrections: Weight forward on the pivot
Arms to first then open to fourth faster to get around
Bottom leg turns out fully
Glissade: point second foot especially devant; barely elevate but have a sense of lift
Step-unfold: currently unstable — cause not yet identified
Eyeline: wider fourth position; follows the hands; more peripheral space
Back heel down in the back bend
Move upper body over the toes in derrière
Don't contract the neck

exercises.name: Centre practice and pirouettes
corrections: Pirouettes closing in 3rd not 5th — axis collapses before rotation completes
Arm-driven rotation rather than hip-initiated; shoulder wind-up especially in en dedans
Pirouette preparation: weight not fully forward in fondu — dorsiflexion restriction is load-dependent, not structural
Passé dropping at 1.5 turns mid-rotation — anchor failing on transition to second turn
Heels landing at same time rather than dropping into closed fifth
Think going up in the turn; lock standing knee and pull up

exercises.name: Pirouette enchaînement
corrections: Same pirouette axis and arm-compensation issues as AF-10
Lame duck: compact fourth under the body not fifth; hips to the corner — pelvis rotating with arms not staying square
Lame ducks: not tight, weight back
En dedans: back leg not coming in fast enough — passé retiré delay
Left pose turns too slow

exercises.name: Adage
corrections: Arabesque: second shoulder hiking — outer hip and side body not stabilising torso
Arabesque: eyeline down toward floor
Arabesque: back compresses and grips at end range — hip flexor ceiling, not back strength problem
Fouetté en l'air: deep hip rotators releasing too early before arabesque extension
Arms need continuous movement through adage for fluidity
Pull up on standing leg; lock standing knee for stability

exercises.name: Variation 2 (currently restricted — no pointe)
corrections: Poses: posterior weight bias, not getting over the box; outer ankle not firing before weight transfer
Poses: retiré not getting up fast enough
Pas de bourrées: traveling rather than turning on the spot — retiré not at full height
Eyeline: up and over, not throwing the head back
Épaulement: more upper back twist

exercises.name: Discovering Repertoire
corrections: Chaînes: half-turn half-turn rhythmic clarity; heels close; don't lean back; spot faster
Grand jetés: better battement; look up and over not at the ground
Développé devant: square shoulders and hips
Musicality is the main priority across the class
Projection: look beyond the arms and shoulders
Fourth arms: shoulders straight, not tilted to either side
Jeté passé: compact and directional, doesn't travel
À la seconde extension: active flexibility not just passive stretch

exercises.name: Allegro (currently restricted — glissades only)
corrections: Sharper beats from top of thighs — turnout collapsing mid-air
Articulate feet; weight forward at push-off
Ballonné: gesture foot not snapping to full point at apex — active pointing, not passive stretch
Stamina collapse across allegro sections — conditioning gap, not purely technique

itemsToRaise: Right ankle plié range still restricted and not improving — is there an active intervention or is it purely time?
Request rough clearance timeline for relevés, pointe, and allegro — needed for May/June 2027 exam planning
Left ankle continuing to overload from right ankle compensation — 15+ weeks of cumulative compensatory load
Bilateral winging on demi-pointe: correctable with strengthening or structural finding?
physioNotes.clearanceStatus: Right ankle grade 2 ligament tear — approx 15 weeks post-injury (injured mid-March 2026). Current restrictions: no pointe, no jumps (glissades permitted), no sharp relevés on flat. Returning to class with modified participation.
restrictions: Cleared: Bilateral parallel pliés and rises at barre (slow, controlled)
Cleared: Single-leg static balance (monitor for soreness)
